rating_reasoning.summary: Owns the AI compute brains layer (GR00T, Cosmos, Jetson Thor). Watch (not Strong) because the data-center thesis is already in price; humanoid is real optionality but won't move the needle for years.
primary_drivers: GPU near-monopoly with CUDA software moat — winner of memory-bandwidth and compute-density bottlenecks.
Brains layer for Physical AI: GR00T N1.6 + Cosmos Reason 2 + Isaac Lab-Arena + Jetson Thor reference design.
Tier 2 in humanoid report — humanoid is a free option, not the marginal driver of the stock today.
key_risks: Forward P/E 21.6-25.8x cheap given growth — but the data-center thesis is fully attributable, not surprising.
Humanoid contribution is <1% of revenue; rerating happens by attribution, not surprise.
Inference shift toward custom ASICs (hyperscaler in-house silicon) over time.

evidence.ref_title: Physical AI / Humanoid Robotics Supply Chain: Where the Mispricings Still Live
evidence.report_reasoning: Tier 2. Owns the brains layer (GR00T N1.6 model, Cosmos Reason 2, Isaac Lab-Arena, Jetson Thor, IGX Thor). Q1 FY27 (May 20 2026 8-K): $81.6B total revenue +85% YoY; Data Center alone $75.2B +92% YoY; Automotive & Robotics combined only ~$2.3B FY26 — robotics is <1% of total. CES 2026 partnerships include Agility, 1X, Boston Dynamics, Hexagon, Wandelbots, NEURA, Caterpillar, Franka. Forward P/E 21.6-25.8x cheap given growth, but upside is data center, not humanoid. Humanoid is a free option but won't move the needle for years. Own NVDA for the data-center story; humanoid by attribution, not surprise.

evidence.physics_summary: Inference is memory-bandwidth-bound, not compute-bound. Each token generation requires loading the full model weights from HBM to SRAM.

Modern AI accelerators draw 1,000+ amps at sub-1V supply rails. At Rubin-class power densities (~1,800W/GPU, Rubin Ultra targeting ~3,600W), IR drop (voltage loss to resistance through the power distribution network) reached ~7% on conventional front-side power delivery — meaning ~7% of supplied power was wasted heating the interconnect before reaching transistors.

evidence.ref_name: Compute Density at the Reticle Limit
evidence.physics_summary: Modern AI accelerators are reticle-limited — the maximum die area that can be patterned in a single EUV exposure is ~858 mm² at current ASML reticle limits. To pack more transistors into a single accelerator package, designers must either shrink the process node (TSMC N3 → N2 → A16 → A14, each step costing ~$15-30B in new fab capex) or stack multiple dies in 3D via hybrid bonding.

evidence.ref_name: Edge AI Silicon for Humanoid Robots
evidence.physics_summary: Each humanoid robot requires a stack of silicon: a high-TOPS edge AI SoC for vision and planning (NVIDIA Jetson Thor delivers 2,000+ FP4 TFLOPS; Qualcomm Dragonwing IQ10 700 TOPS in an 18-core Oryon CPU; Ambarella CV7 with 2.5× the AI performance of CV5 at 8K vision resolution), sensor-fusion ICs (mmWave radar like TI's IWR6243, LiDAR time-of-flight, force/torque sensors at every joint), motor control ICs (typically 30-50 actuators per humanoid at $5-15 in BOM each), and power management distributed across the body. ADI sells signal-chain + power + connectivity at every joint and subsystem as a default supplier given its breadth — industrial is 50% of ADI's revenue.

binding_constraint_thesis: The binding constraint on AI infrastructure scale-out is training-compute throughput × inference-memory bandwidth × developer ecosystem (CUDA software lock-in), and NVDA owns all three; every alternative (custom ASIC, AMD MI300X, Trainium) ships into a market NVDA defines — chokepoint, not a beneficiary.

Stage-7 chokepoint thesis required before any CORE designation; current rating capped at SWING.
Hard stop $119.03 (52-week low) — entry must be sized against a real exit trigger.

evidence.ref_title: Theme-to-Pick Pipeline Re-Run — Shay Boloor 2027 Software Comps
evidence.report_reasoning: 5/5 pillars. Q1 2026 (May 4 2026 per SEC 8-K): revenue +85% YoY to $1.633B; full-year guide raised 10 points to $7.65-7.66B ('largest ever raise'); Rule of 40 of 145%; adj FCF margin 57% (true economic margin closer to 35-40% on SBC-adjusted basis). EV/NTM Sales 41x against ~50% forward 3-yr CAGR = EV/S ÷ CAGR = 0.82 (passes <2.5 PEG-equivalent gate easily, earns 20 asymmetry points for 'cheap vs own growth' at $321B market cap). Counter-signal: stock fell the day after the print — multiple compression regime. SWING-only entry, ≤2% of risk budget. Hard stop: daily close below $119.03 52-week low OR breach of 200-day SMA on a weekly close. Time stop: if Q2 (August) print does not show U.S. commercial growth ≥110% YoY (vs the 133% just printed), exit regardless of price. Required precondition: user must articulate Stage-7 chokepoint thesis — is Foundry/AIP the governance-and-ontology layer enterprises must license to deploy agentic AI on regulated data, OR a high-margin beneficiary that only re-rates if the true chokepoint name (NVDA-style compute or a frontier model lab) re-rates first? Without that thesis, PLTR sits as PASS, not SWING.

evidence.ref_title: Marvell Technology (MRVL) — Exhaustive Deep-Dive & Theme-to-Pick Verdict
evidence.report_reasoning: Tier-2 AI-connectivity beneficiary with real franchise: AWS Trainium 2 in production (Marvell exclusive on T2), Microsoft Maia in design/preproduction, 800G/1.6T PAM4 DSPs at 60-80% share, Celestial AI Photonic Fabric chiplets for CPO-era repositioning (Tier-2 → Tier-1.5). Q4 FY26 (March 5 2026): $2.219B revenue +22%, data center $1.65B (74% of mix). Q1 FY27 guide $2.4B ±5%. But Stage-7 chokepoint analysis: MRVL is a beneficiary, NOT a chokepoint — binding constraints in 18-36 months are TSMC advanced packaging, HBM (sold out 2026 at all three vendors), power/grid, and NVIDIA GPU + Broadcom switch-ASIC capacity. Disputed AWS T3/T4 socket (Benchmark Dec 8 2025: 'high conviction lost to Alchip Mariana'; JPMorgan/Marvell mgmt counter-narrative). Verdict PASS/SWING-only because Phase 5 = automatic PASS regardless of pillar count; founder-led FAIL caps maximum designation at SWING (never CORE). Next true catalyst window: Q1 FY27 earnings May 27, 2026 — every figure could change within 9 days. Entering before earnings on a Phase 5 setup is the textbook framework violation.

evidence.physics_summary: At 800Gbps and 1.6Tbps per lane, copper signals fall apart after a few feet; data must be converted to laser light via optical transceivers — thumb-drive-sized devices containing tiny lasers that switch state in well under a nanosecond with extremely consistent timing. PAM4 modulation (4-level pulse amplitude) crams 2 bits into each pulse, doubling the data rate without doubling laser switching speed.

evidence.ref_title: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI): A Plain-English Deep Dive Into the AI Optical Networking Story
evidence.report_reasoning: Picks-and-shovels play on 800G→1.6T optical ramp. Bull case: vertical InP laser fab is the moat that protected AAOI during 2024-2025 industry-wide laser shortages (competitors buy from Lumentum/Coherent); Amazon warrant up to $4B through 2035 converts customer to long-term equity-aligned partner; disclosed >$324M 800G/1.6T orders ($200M+ first 1.6T from one customer, $124M cumulative 800G from another); $1.4B mid-2027 annualized run-rate from 1.6T alone; US manufacturing (>55% by end-2027) aligns with hyperscaler supply-chain resilience preference. Bear case: customer concentration recreates the 2017 dynamic that destroyed the stock; Innolight 'projected to capture 50-60% of the 1.6T module market in 2025' per Deep Fundamental Substack — head-start may already be narrower than it looks; Citron Research April 10 2026 short call ('paying beyond monopoly multiples for commodity economics' at 112x forward earnings); CPO threat 2028-2030 from Broadcom Bailly 51.2T (reduces optics power 65% — 15W pluggable vs 5.4W per 800G); GAAP losses continue (-$14.3M Q1 2026, -$38.2M FY 2025); $600M ATM means dilution will continue. Sized for risk: 1-2% starter on weakness to $100-130; max 4-5% diversified portfolio (5-year beta 3.76 — when SPY falls 1%, AAOI falls ~3.76%).

evidence.physics_summary: At 800Gbps and 1.6Tbps per lane, copper signals fall apart after a few feet; data must be converted to laser light via optical transceivers — thumb-drive-sized devices containing tiny lasers that switch state in well under a nanosecond with extremely consistent timing. PAM4 modulation (4-level pulse amplitude) crams 2 bits into each pulse, doubling the data rate without doubling laser switching speed.

evidence.ref_title: Tempus AI (TEM) Swing Trade Plan — Bucket 2 Framework
evidence.report_reasoning: Precision-medicine AI play. Q1 2026 revenue $348.1M +36.1% YoY (per Business Wire May 5 2026); adj EBITDA -$2.8M (-0.8% margin); FY26 guide raised to $1.59-1.60B. Bristol Myers Squibb expanded collaboration May 14 across 5 oncology + neuroscience clinical programs using Tempus Lens. Pre-positioning signals: ARK accumulated ~620K shares May 6-15 (~$30M), TEM is ARK's third-largest holding (~5% of portfolio); short interest 27.03% of 99.01M float (5.33 days-to-cover) — squeeze fuel on positive Investor Day surprise; IV30 in 12th percentile of 1-year range (cheap implied vol); P/C OI 0.67 (modestly bullish). Insider context: CEO Lefkofsky sold 166,250 Class A shares April 28 at ~$51 under 10b5-1 plan adopted March 4 2025 (mechanical, not discretionary); NO open-market insider buys in last 30 days. SWING-only because high volatility, pending class actions, Rule-of-40 fails ≥40 hurdle. Trade structure: $46.50 primary entry on confirmed close ≥ $46.28 with ≥6M volume; hard stop $39.50; +50% trim at $69.75 (convert conversion price), +100% trim at $93.00 (capped-call cap); 180-day time stop ~Nov 16-18 2026.
